name: Paygent Connect
one_liner: Paygent Connect is a white-label, NMI-powered payment gateway for merchants/ISOs to process cards and manage transactions, exposing NMI's Payment API for integration.
primary_auth: API key (NMI 'security_key' issued per merchant account)
access_notes: Paygent Connect is a rebranded instance of NMI's white-label gateway. There is no public self-serve signup; a merchant account must be provisioned through the ISO/reseller that operates the branded gateway. NMI itself offers an Integration Portal/Sandbox, but obtaining a working production account and per-merchant security_key requires a sales/partner relationship. No independent 'Paygent Connect' developer portal or free API tier was found.
api_notes: Underlying platform is NMI (docs.nmi.com). Integration surfaces include the Payment API / Direct Post (form-encoded HTTP POST), Collect.js and Three-Step Redirect for tokenized/hosted card entry, and a Query API for reporting. Operations cover sale, auth, capture, void, refund, credit, recurring/subscriptions, and Customer Vault (stored payment methods), plus invoicing. Estimated a few dozen operations, hence MODERATE. Exact endpoint set for the Paygent Connect brand not separately documented; assumed to mirror standard NMI.
mcp_notes: No MCP server found for 'Paygent Connect' or for NMI shipped by the vendor. Searches returned no vendor or community MCP implementation.
main_blocker: Credentials are gated: no self-serve signup for the branded gateway, and a live per-merchant NMI security_key requires a merchant account provisioned via the ISO/reseller. The API itself (NMI) is well-documented and integration-friendly once you have keys.

evidence.claim: NMI offers a white-label payment gateway that ISOs/partners rebrand with their own logo, colors and URL, with access via a sales/partner relationship.
NMI provides Payment API/SDK developer documentation and a sandbox integration portal for building payment solutions.
A 'Paygent Connect' gateway integration is referenced with merchant ConnectID/ConnectPassword-style credentials in a third-party library.

open_questions: Ambiguity between (a) 'Paygent Connect' as a white-label NMI gateway brand (matching the NMI-powered hint) and (b) the Japanese 'Paygent' payment service (paygent.co.jp), which is not NMI-based and uses ConnectID/ConnectPassword. Following the NMI-powered hint, this record assumes (a). The specific reseller operating the Paygent Connect brand and its exact merchant-onboarding path were not found; confirmation of a dedicated developer portal or sandbox for this brand is needed.
